concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains an agency logo ('NC Water Resources Environmental Quality') on the top left of the first page. According to the provided Image Manifest, the extracted base64 data for this image (and the footer gradient image) is corrupt ('valid': false, error: 'No module named \'PIL\''). Because the image data is invalid, the tag in the HTML will result in a broken image icon. Since this is an agency logo on the first page, its failure to render constitutes a CRITICAL defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC Water Resources Environmental Quality logo on the top left of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: Image manifest shows 'valid': false for the image with alt text starting 'Logo featuring the letters 'NC'...', meaning the image is broken.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (5): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 5; Page 3 of 5
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=5. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document utilizes bold and underlined text to emphasize the leading topics in the bulleted lists on pages 3 and 4 (e.g., 'Agriculture', 'Air Quality', 'Beneficial Reuse'). The output entirely strips this underline emphasis, presenting the terms as plain text without any markup.

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
